ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Agricultural Scientists develop and implement sustainable agricultural practices to improve crop yields and reduce environmental impact.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Agricultural Scientists design and conduct experiments to test the effects of different variables on crop growth and development. They analyze data and develop models to predict crop yields and recommend best practices for farmers.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: M.Sc. in Agriculture or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in pursuing a career in agriculture can start by taking science and math courses, such as biology, chemistry, and physics.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can bridge into the field by taking relevant certifications and courses, such as agricultural extension or farm management.

future_builder.description: Agricultural Scientists with experience can advance their careers by taking on leadership roles, pursuing higher education, or specializing in a specific area, such as plant breeding or soil science.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2-3 years of experience as a Junior Agricultural Scientist

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into agricultural science by developing relevant skills, such as data analysis, programming, and communication.

progression.senior_level: Senior Agricultural Scientist or Research Manager (5+ yrs)

roadmap.title: Data Analysis and Programming
topics: Data Analysis with R or Python
